title: Error activating virtual environment in Execute Command node
symptom: When trying to activate a Python virtual environment using the Execute Command node, n8n throws an error stating 'source: not found'. This prevents the script from running as expected.
applies_when: Trying to activate a Python virtual environment
not_this_if: Using a different shell or command execution method
root_cause: The Execute Command node in n8n uses /bin/sh by default, which does not support the 'source' command as it is a bash-specific built-in. This leads to the error when attempting to activate a virtual environment.
fix_steps: Replace 'source' with a dot (.) in the command to activate the virtual environment.
Update the command in the Execute Command node to '. /path/to/your/venv/bin/activate'.
agent_instruction: Change 'source' to '.' in your command to activate the virtual environment.
